stimulus: In a study of the evolution of cnc-X and cnc-Y—genes encoding antimicrobial peptides that combat pathogens and foster beneficial microbes in the nematode Caenorhabditis elegans—researchers assessed worm resistance to infections by Microbacterium nematophilum and Serratia marcescens, two bacteria common in worm environments. Subjects included worms identified by mutations silencing cnc-X, cnc-Y, or both cnc-X and cnc-Y (termed types X, Y, and XY, respectively). In conjunction with the observation that resistance to M. nematophilum correlates with cnc-Y activity but is not significantly affected by cnc-X activity, data on survival rates following infection with S. marcescens suggest that ______
stem: Which completion of the text is best supported by the data?

answerOptions.content: cnc-X may have developed as a specific defense against S. marcescens.
cnc-Y confers defense against S. marcescens regardless of the presence of cnc-X.
cnc-X protects against only one bacteria species, whereas cnc-Y protects against multiple species.
defense against S. marcescens is strongest when both cnc-X and cnc-Y are present.

rationale: Choice A is correct. The text states that resistance to M. nematophilum tracks cnc-Y activity but not cnc-X activity. The table shows that after S. marcescens infection, type X worms (with cnc-X silenced but cnc-Y active) die rapidly, whereas type Y worms (with cnc-Y silenced but cnc-X active) survive at high rates; worms with both silenced (type XY) also die rapidly. This pattern indicates that cnc-X activity is required for resistance to S. marcescens, suggesting cnc-X may have evolved as a specific defense against that pathogen. Choice B is incorrect because the data show survival is poor whenever cnc-X is silenced, regardless of cnc-Y, so cnc-Y does not confer defense against S. marcescens. Choice C is incorrect because only two bacteria are discussed; the data do not support a count of multiple species. Choice D is incorrect because no group with both genes simultaneously active is presented; all groups in the table have one or both genes silenced.
